back_translation: At that time, the Lord said to me: Chisel for yourself two stone tablets like the first, and come up to Me to the mountain, and you shall make for yourself an ark of wood.
And I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke, and you shall place them in the ark.
And I made an ark of acacia wood, and I chiseled two stone tablets like the first, and I went up the mountain, and the two tablets were in my hand.

And He wrote on the tablets according to the first writing, the Ten Commandments that the Lord spoke to you on the mountain from the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly. And the Lord gave them to me.
And I turned and went down from the mountain and placed the tablets in the ark that I had made, and they were there as the Lord commanded me.
And the sons of Israel traveled from Beeroth Bene-jaakan to Moserah. There Aaron died, and he was buried there, and Eleazar his son served as priest in his place.
From there they traveled to Gudgodah, and from Gudgodah to Jotbathah, a land of streams of water.
At that time the Lord set apart the tribe of Levi to carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord, to stand before the Lord to serve Him, and to bless in His name, until this day.
Therefore, Levi has no portion and inheritance with his brothers; the Lord, He is his inheritance, as the Lord your God spoke to him.
And I stood on the mountain like the first days, forty days and forty nights, and the Lord listened to me also that time; the Lord did not desire to destroy you.
And the Lord said to me: Arise, go for the journey before the people, and they shall enter and possess the land that I swore to their fathers to give to them.

And now, Israel, what does the Lord your God ask of you, except to fear the Lord your God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him, and to serve the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul,
to keep the commandments of the Lord and His statutes that I am commanding you today for your good.
Behold, to the Lord your God belong the heavens and the heavens of the heavens, the earth and all that is in it.
Only in your fathers did the Lord delight to love them, and He chose in their seed after them, in you, from all the peoples, as this day.
And you shall circumcise the foreskin of your heart, and your neck you shall not stiffen any longer.
For the Lord your God, He is God of gods and Lord of lords, the great God, the mighty and the awesome, who does not lift up faces and does not take a bribe.
Doer of justice for the fatherless and the widow, and lover of the stranger, to give him bread and clothing.
And you shall love the stranger, for strangers you were in the land of Egypt.
The Lord your God you shall fear; Him you shall serve, and to Him you shall cling, and in His name you shall swear.
He is your praise, and He is your God, who has done with you these great and awesome things that your eyes have seen.
With seventy souls your fathers went down to Egypt, and now the Lord your God has made you as the stars of the heavens in multitude.
